name: Lecanemab reduced amyloid burden and slowed early Alzheimer's decline
summary: Clarity AD provides direct human evidence that antibody-mediated amyloid-beta lowering can slow decline in early Alzheimer's disease, with ARIA and infusion-reaction caveats.
statement: In early Alzheimer's disease, lecanemab reduced amyloid PET burden and produced moderately less cognitive and functional decline than placebo at 18 months.
population_or_model: Adults aged 50 to 90 years with early Alzheimer's disease and amyloid evidence
quantitative_note: CDR-SB change was 1.21 with lecanemab versus 1.66 with placebo, difference -0.45 (95% CI -0.67 to -0.23; P<0.001); amyloid PET burden fell by 59.1 centiloids more than placebo in the substudy.
caveats: The benefit is disease-specific and modest rather than a broad aging or proteostasis-restoration outcome.
Lecanemab caused infusion reactions in 26.4% of participants and amyloid-related imaging abnormalities with edema or effusion in 12.6%.
The study does not establish long-term dementia prevention, mortality benefit, or generalized aggregate clearance outside amyloid-beta disease.
